text: right, people. You have your team assignments, and you have your objectives. We’ll have an FOB established to the west of town, where we can evacuate the injured to be taken care of by Nancy’s people. Alpha team will head over the wall and secure the hospital, from which they’ll push into the bot district. That should create enough trouble to call in Warlord’s bots from outside the area . “While they’re distracted, Lara will attempt to rally human support. Bravo team, you’re on her like flies on shit. If she’s successful, Bravo will escort her and Newton into the Market, and he will attempt to sway the bots to our cause .” “The human settlement is usually clear,” Lara said. “Gearheads only go there randomly, looking for rulebreakers to punish or desperate people to toy with. Otherwise, we could suck dirt for all the shits they give. The hard part is going to be getting into the Market without raising suspicion .” Rodriguez pressed his palms to the tabletop, fingers splayed, and took a slow, deep breath. “There are a lot of moving parts here, and we’re relying on some pretty big variables. We have no guarantee that any of these people, flesh or metal, will support us. I don’t have to remind any of you that this is the most significant operation any of us has ever attempted. This isn’t a scrap run into the wasteland. This is a war . Our goal is to win it without anyone getting hurt, but best-case isn’t what you plan for in these situations .” Rodriguez tilted his head towards Captain Cooper, who leaned forward and took over. “We’ve scouted this place thoroughly over the last few weeks. Security’s lax, but we’ve identified at least twenty-five unique tangos, equipped with various small arms. They are all confirmed as bots. We don’t know if any of them have reinforced casings, so we must assume that our weaponry will only be effective within about fifty yards . “We’re not going to be able to use radios. Most bots have internal antennas, Anderson says because they used to connect to wireless networks, or something. Anyway, that means that they’ll be able to detect our communications…so we’re going to have to listen for gunfire as our signal . “Stick to your squads, watch each other’s backs, and be mindful of cover. We don’t want to lose anyone, but it’s going to be real bullets when they return fire, and none of us are bulletproof .” It only took one shot in the right spot to take down a bot. Reinforced casings couldn’t handle the armor-piercing ammunition that was deployed in the latter days of the War, according to Newton. Ronin had dealt with enough holes to know the truth of it . “And what about Warlord?” Lara asked . “We’ve had a positive ID, but he doesn’t seem to stick to a particular habit. Goes to Kitty’s a few nights a week, never at the same time. We’re hoping all this will flush him out,” Cooper replied . “He’ll come,” she said. “Bastard likes to take care of things himself, usually. Problem is, will he go hide once he realizes what’s happening ?” “I believe we can provide a little insight,” William said, clearing his throat. “I don’t know how much use it will be , but …” “We have to treat a portion of this mission as a manhunt.” Rodriguez’s expression was stoic, his gaze shielded. “Warlord must be captured or deactivated. Any intel will be useful .” William nodded, gesturing to Newton. The bot leaned forward, metallic fingers gliding over a small console inlaid in the table. The lights in the room dimmed, and a screen came on behind Rodriguez, slowly gaining brightness . “Newton and I have been, with Nancy’s help, delving into the old records on file here. A good portion of the data is corrupted and inaccessible, and most of what we can access is irrelevant, but we’ve found some information on Warlord that may be pertinent to understanding his state of mind .” A grainy, pixelated image appeared on the screen. Rodriguez slid his chair aside . On the screen, a man sat at a table in an office. The image clarified as the recording played . Lara grasped Ronin’s hand, squeezing . The man was Warlord . He was bald, cheeks gaunt, and dark circles cradled his sunken eyes, but it was unmistakably Cheyenne’s tyrannical ruler . “You understand the risks involved, Mr. Turner?” a female, off-camera, asked. Her voice crackled over the unseen speakers in the war room . Kevin Turner, the man who would become Warlord, laughed bitterly. “Yeah. But, look at me, Doctor Yuan. Death isn’t a risk, it’s an inevitability. If this means a little more time with my family…” He dropped his gaze and ran his tongue over his lips . “In all honesty, Mr. Turner — ” “Kevin, please. I don’t really have it in me to deal with formalities anymore, you know ?” “Kevin. In all honesty, the chances of this procedure being successful are very small. I have to warn you that you might prematurely end what time you have remaining with them .” He shook his head, sliding a palm over the surface of the table absently. “Maybe. But what more time do I have with them right now? A few weeks for them to watch me die? At least this way, it’s on my terms. And they don’t have to …” Turning away from the camera, Kevin lifted a trembling hand to his face, covering his eyes as tears welled. “They don’t have to see me suffering. Don’t have to watch me become a shell of the man I was, and…and my family won’t have to feel so damned helpless while it all happens .” When he dropped his hand, moisture glistened in his eyes, and there were splotches of color on his cheeks. He faced the camera
